title: To .... .... On Seeing Her With A White Veil And A Rich Girdle.
content:
Put off the vestal Veil, nor, oh!
        Let weeping angels View it;
    Your cheeks belie its virgin snow.
        And blush repenting through it.

    Put off the fatal zone you wear;
        The shining pearls around it
    Are tears, that fell from Virtue there,
        The hour when Love unbound it.
meaning:
Thomas Moore's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To .... .... On Seeing Her With A White Veil And A Rich Girdle."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
